title: <div>- Emergence patterns: Where did unexpected beauty happen? Where did the sys
content:
- Emergence patterns: Where did unexpected beauty happen? Where did the system surprise you both? What did the audience do that you didn’t anticipate?
- Writer’s workflow: What did you need from the technical infrastructure to do your best thinking? What should future writers ask for? What should they avoid?
- Governance and creative authority: How do performers maintain directorial control? How do you explain weighting algorithms to audiences? How do you make audience input feel consequential without being destabilizing?

Format: Collaborative documentation. We write this together—not me documenting your work, but both of us articulating what we learned. Probably a 15–20 page essayistic document + visual diagrams showing parameter mapping, decision trees, and case studies from what we built.

Your contribution: Dramaturgical articulation. You help future writers understand not just what happened, but why it worked or didn’t. You write the writer’s perspective in the handbook. You become the voice of practitioner wisdom in the system architecture.

Outcome: A “Narrative Design Handbook for Participatory Performance”—this becomes the onboarding document for every writer/storyteller I work with after you. When I recruit the next dramaturgist, I hand them this document and say: “Here’s how Chris thought about this. Here’s what he discovered. Here’s what you should build on.”
